title: A working model of neural activity and phenomenal experience in psychosis.

assets.abstract: According to classical phenomenology, phenomenal experience is composed of perceptions (related to environmental stimuli) and imagery/ideas (unrelated to environmental stimuli). Intensity/vividness is supposed to represent the key phenomenal difference between perceptions and ideas, higher in perceptions than ideas, and thus the core subjective criterion to distinguish reality from imagination. At a neural level, phenomenal experience is related to brain activity in the sensory areas, driven by
